description: Vectorized NumPy array access for ball-ball contact attributes.

functions.name: branch
functions.description: Get a numpy array of the contact branch vectors in the global coordinate system.

examples.code:
import itasca as it
values = it.ballballarray.branch()

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.

functions.name: extra
functions.signature: itasca.ballballarray.extra (slot : int) -> array float{contact} or float{contact,3}.
functions.description: Get the contact extra data in the given slot as an array. Extra variables accessed by array must be of type float or vec.

examples.code:
import itasca as it
values = it.ballballarray.extra()

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
Array extra variables must be float or vector values in the selected slot.

functions.name: fill_branch
functions.description: Fill an existing array with the contact branch vectors in the global coordinate system. The array must be the correct shape.

examples.code:
import numpy as np
import itasca as it
data = np.empty_like(it.ballballarray.branch())
it.ballballarray.fill_branch(data)
examples.description: Fill a preallocated NumPy array to avoid allocating a new result array.

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
The destination array must already have the correct shape and dtype.

functions.name: indices
functions.description: Get a numpy array of the indices of the balls on end1 and end2 of the contacts. The ball indices are relative to the ball arrays returned by the itasca.ballarray module.

examples.code:
import itasca as it
values = it.ballballarray.indices()

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.

functions.name: set_extra
functions.signature: itasca.ballballarray.set_extra (slot: int, data: array float{contact} or float{contact, 3}) -> None.
functions.description: Set the contact extra data in the given slot with an array. Extra variables set by array must be of type float or vec.

examples.code:
import itasca as it
values = it.ballballarray.set_extra()

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
Setter functions modify model state immediately.
Array extra variables must be float or vector values in the selected slot.

functions.name: set_group
functions.signature: itasca.ballballarray.set_group (membership: array bool{contact}, group_name: str, slot=1) -> None.
functions.description: Set contact group from an array. Where membership True set the corresponding contact to be a member of group group_name in the given slot.

examples.code:
import itasca as it
values = it.ballballarray.set_group()

notes: Array APIs operate on all current objects of this type and return or accept NumPy arrays with the shape shown in the signature.
Setter functions modify model state immediately.
